channel.title: Is there an ability to fetch Yoast data separately from the page?
channel.description:
I have a [project that uses subdomains as custom post type endpoints](https://community.frontity.org/t/how-to-set-frontity-homepage-independent-of-wordpress-home-page/3376/8?u=david1) in my Frontity theme. However, because the route for these subdomains/custom post types is always `/`, I am only seeing `<head>` data corresponding to the home page of the website. I can see that the Yoast data is working for other pages:
* [Home page](https://microbeninja.com) * [Contact Us page](https://microbeninja.com/contact-us/) (different from Home page) * [Microsite/Subdomain page](https://annihilyzer.microbeninja.com) (same as Home page but should be the same as [this post in the back end](https://wpadmin.microbeninja.com/microsite/annihilyzer/))
Is there a way I can just pull the Yoast data from a different route than what is being served? Is this the right way to do this? Should I be using the `head-tags` package instead? Thanks for any help! Below is a link to the repo:
https://gitlab.artlyticalmedia.com/artlytical-media/microbe-ninja-frontity-theme

item.title: Is there an ability to fetch Yoast data separately from the page?
item.dc:creator: codemonkeynorth
item.description:
if you wanted to get the full yoast_head you can do something along the lines of

theme’s index.js

beforeSSR: async ({ state, actions, libraries }) => {
  if(state.router.link === '/') 
    // make sure contact page is loaded into our state
    await actions.source.fetch('/contact')
  }
}

site’s index.js

// get the contact data we grabbed in beforeSSR
const contact_data = state.source.get('/contact-us')
const contact_post = state.source[contact_data.type][contact_data.id];  
console.log(contact_poast.yoast_head) // full yoast_head string

I don’t know you get individual items out of yoast though, other than parsing that string

Read full topic
item.source: Is there an ability to fetch Yoast data separately from the page?

item.title: Is there an ability to fetch Yoast data separately from the page?
item.dc:creator: david1
item.description:
I have a project that uses subdomains as custom post type endpoints in my Frontity theme. However, because the route for these subdomains/custom post types is always /, I am only seeing <head> data corresponding to the home page of the website. I can see that the Yoast data is working for other pages:

  • Home page
  • Contact Us page (different from Home page)
  • Microsite/Subdomain page (same as Home page but should be the same as this post in the back end)

Is there a way I can just pull the Yoast data from a different route than what is being served? Is this the right way to do this? Should I be using the head-tags package instead? Thanks for any help! Below is a link to the repo:

GitLab

Artlytical Media / microbe-ninja-frontity-theme

GitLab Enterprise Edition

Read full topic
item.source: Is there an ability to fetch Yoast data separately from the page?
